meta.name: Chocolate Cake Slices (Bakery-Style Portioned)
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
meta.description: A bakery-style chocolate sheet cake designed specifically for clean portioned slices. Built with slice logic: stable crumb, level bake, chill-and-cut workflow, and a simple ganache-style glaze that sets cleanly for display. Includes both commercial flour and fresh-milled flour guidance (choose one).

ingredients.label: Commercial flour option (use this OR the fresh-milled option): all-purpose flour
Fresh-milled flour option (use this OR the commercial option): fine-milled, sifted
ingredients.notes: Fresh-milled absorbs more and can add texture; sift coarse bran for cleaner slice crumb. Batter may need +15–45 ml extra buttermilk/coffee.

ingredients.label: Hot coffee (preferred) or hot water
ingredients.notes: About 1 cup. Hot liquid blooms cocoa and improves chocolate flavor.

ingredients.label: Extra hot coffee/water or buttermilk (for fresh-milled adjustment)
ingredients.notes: Add 15 ml at a time if batter is too thick; up to ~45 ml total depending on flour.

ingredients.label: Semi-sweet chocolate (chips or chopped) for ganache glaze
Light corn syrup or honey (optional, for shine and softer set)
ingredients.notes: About 1 tbsp. Optional but gives bakery-style sheen.

steps.title: Prep pan and oven (slice logic)
steps.body: Heat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line a 9x13 pan with parchment with overhang on two sides for easy lifting. Lightly grease parchment. This enables clean lift-out and clean slice edges.

steps.title: Choose your flour option
steps.body: Use either the commercial flour option OR the fresh-milled flour option (not both). For fresh-milled flour, fine-mill and sift coarse bran for a cleaner slice crumb.

steps.title: Mix dry ingredients
steps.body: In a large bowl, whisk chosen flour, cocoa powder, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.

steps.title: Mix wet ingredients
steps.body: In a medium bowl, whisk eggs, oil, buttermilk, and vanilla.

steps.title: Combine wet into dry
steps.body: Pour wet mixture into dry and whisk until mostly smooth.

steps.title: Add hot coffee/water (cocoa bloom)
steps.body: Whisk in hot coffee/water slowly. Batter will be thin—this is correct for a moist, even crumb that slices cleanly.

steps.title: Adjust batter for fresh-milled flour (if needed)
steps.body: If using fresh-milled flour and batter is thicker than expected, add extra hot coffee/water or buttermilk 15 ml at a time until batter is pourable. Rest 5 minutes to hydrate, then whisk gently once more.

steps.title: Bake
steps.body: Pour batter into prepared pan. Bake 26–34 minutes until a toothpick comes out with a few moist crumbs (not wet batter). Do not overbake—overbaking dries slices.

steps.title: Cool, then chill for clean slicing (bakery portion logic)
steps.body: Cool in pan 20 minutes, then lift cake out using parchment and cool completely. For the cleanest slices, chill the cake (wrapped) 1–2 hours before glazing and cutting.

steps.title: Make ganache glaze
steps.body: Place chocolate in a bowl. Heat cream to just simmering (not boiling). Pour over chocolate, add optional corn syrup/honey and salt, let stand 2 minutes, then stir until smooth and glossy.

steps.title: Glaze and set
steps.body: Pour ganache over chilled cake and spread evenly. Chill 20–40 minutes until glaze is set but not rock-hard (for clean cuts without cracking).

steps.title: Portion into bakery slices
steps.body: Trim edges for perfectly squared presentation (optional). Cut into 12 large slices (3x4 grid) or 16 standard slices (4x4 grid). Use a long knife warmed in hot water and wiped dry between cuts.

notes: Portioned cake logic: bake flat, cool fully, chill, then glaze and cut. Chilling firms crumb and glaze for clean edges.
Hot-liquid chocolate cakes look thin but bake up moist and even—ideal for slices.
Fresh-milled flour: sift coarse bran for cleaner slice; batter may need a small extra liquid adjustment and a brief rest.
If you plan to display slices individually, chill slices before wrapping for the neatest packaging.

storage.refrigerator: Airtight: up to 5 days. Bring to room temp for softer crumb.
storage.freezer: Freeze unglazed cake or individually wrapped slices up to 2 months. Thaw overnight in refrigerator.
